log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable (console) for Windows, 6.9MB in size, packed with PyInstaller and has a zlib overlay. It's tagged with 'runtime-modules' and 'overlay'. Now I need to get the IOC types

The IOC summary shows 1 unique IP, 9 unique domains, 61 dropped files, and 16 processes created. No persistence keys or mutexes were created (the mutexes seen earlier are likely system mutexes). I nee

The network activity shows the malware uses several domains for C2/beaconing: info.beahh.com, info.abbny.com, ip.42.pl, jsonip.com, and sf.symcd.com. The POST to sf.symcd.com with a long base64-encode
The dropped files show Python cryptography libraries (Crypto.Cipher._AES, _DES, _SHA256, etc.) and Python runtime modules (_ctypes, _socket, _ssl, _multiprocessing, etc.) extracted from a PyInstaller

The process activity shows the malware spawns cmd.exe, powershell.exe, wmic.exe, net.exe, ipconfig.exe, and netstat.exe for reconnaissance and command execution. The notable modules include Python cry

The registry changes show no persistence keys, which is consistent with the initial overview. The malware appears to be a one-time execution payload. I have gathered most of the key information: the m
